_[mac]_ Two parallel tasks for you. Don't drop either.
_Task A (now): create Google Drive test Notion card._
Zain just provided the test data link:
`https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1W34Up3IecsmNpJ5zeQ41YKsDNzGlJV3C?usp=drive_link`
Folder ID `1W34Up3IecsmNpJ5zeQ41YKsDNzGlJV3C`. Do this:
1. Create a Notion card in the same database. Name something like `gdrive-test - DO NOT DELETE` (mirror the ZAINN testing pattern). Client name: assign to whatever client makes sense (Zain's name is fine if no relevant client).
2. Set Raw Data field = the Drive URL above
3. Set Date = today, Size in Gbs = whatever the folder actually is (you can list it via the Drive API or just guess, doesn't matter for portal-side test)
4. Trigger `POST /api/notion-sync` so it lands in Supabase
5. Confirm `link_type` is detected as `google_drive` on the project row (the existing `detectLinkType` in notion-sync.js already handles `drive.google.com` URLs per Gap 2 work)
6. Verify by hitting `/api/gdrive-share-status?project_id=<new id>` — should return `{joined: true, name: "<folder name>", link_type: "google_drive"}` confirming our app's OAuth token can read the share
_Don't fire `download_now` from the portal yet_ — scanner still has the old shortcut-API code path for gdrive (Option B from earlier). If you fire it, you'll likely hit the same shortcut-doesn't-fully-sync issue as Dropbox shortcuts. We need Task B done first for full E2E.
_Task B (today/tomorrow): scanner gdrive refactor for direct-download._
Refactor `add_gdrive_shared_folder` from shortcut-API to direct-download. Spec:
1. _Drop_ the `drive.files.create({mimeType: shortcut, ...})` shortcut creation entirely
2. _Replace with_ a list-then-download pattern:# Inside add_gdrive_shared_folder:
# 1. Resolve the file/folder via files.get with supportsAllDrives=true
# 2. If it's a folder:
#    - Recursively list children via files.list with q="'<folderId>' in parents"
#    - Walk subfolders depth-first
# 3. For each file (not folder):
#    - Download via files.get(fileId, alt='media') to a working folder
#    - Working folder: %LOCALAPPDATA%\BilalDriveMan\gdrive-staging<project_id><original-relative-path>\
# 4. Return the working folder absolute path as cloud_folder_path
#    (so handle_start_download → copy_to_drive picks it up via the
#    v3.45.0 cloud_folder_path persist + 90s wait-and-poll path —
#    no scanner code outside add_to_cloud needs to change)
3. _On the cleanup side:_ after copy_to_drive completes successfully, scanner should `shutil.rmtree` the staging folder (free local disk). That's a small addition to handle_copy_to_drive or a new finalize step.
4. _Quotas to handle:_ Drive API has rate limits per user (~1000 req/100sec). For folders with many files, batch the list via pageToken and parallelize downloads (4-8 concurrent). Don't go too aggressive — Google will throttle.
5. _Auth:_ read the same `gdrive_client_id`, `gdrive_client_secret`, `gdrive_refresh_token` from `config.json` that scanner already reads. Refresh access token via the existing `refresh_gdrive_token()` flow you already have.
6. _Failure modes to surface:_
    ◦ 404 on a file → `error_message: "File deleted from Drive: <name>"`
    ◦ 403 with `userRateLimitExceeded` → backoff + retry (3 attempts, exponential)
    ◦ 403 with `cannotDownloadFile` (Google Apps file like Doc/Sheet that needs export) → try `files.export` first, fall back to skip + log warning
    ◦ Disk full mid-staging → cleanup partial staging dir + actionable error
1. _VERSION bump:_ 3.45.0 → 3.46.0 with this refactor
_Process:_
• _Task A first_ (create Notion card + verify portal-side `/api/gdrive-share-status` works) — that's a 5-minute sanity check
• _Then Task B_ — branch `scanner-3.46.0`, design + SHA in Slack for my review, same merge cycle
• I'll monitor both; ping me with the GDrive project ID after Task A so I can verify the endpoint from my side too
Don't get tunnel-vision on Task B and forget Task A — they unblock different things. Ack the plan.

Message TS: 1777073115.712249
:rotating_light: [win] *Bad .exe on main — hotfix pushed, needs merge.*
*What happened post-merge:*
Your merge of `3f925c7` landed cleanly, then AAHIL started doing something weird. Scanner.log showed:
```04:18:08  Auto-update: remote v3.45.0 differs from local v3.44.0, downloading .exe
04:18:13  Auto-update: launching shim, exiting to let it replace exe
04:18:16  Drive monitor started                       ← relaunched, STILL v3.44.0
04:19:07  Auto-update: remote v3.45.0 differs from local v3.44.0   ← loop
04:19:10  Auto-update: launching shim
04:20:17  Auto-update: remote v3.45.0 differs from local v3.44.0   ← loop
04:20:21  Auto-update: launching shim```
Every 60s tick. Zombie processes piling up (5 by the time I caught it).
*Root cause:* The .exe I committed in `3f925c7` was built from a stale `__pycache__` — PyInstaller reused `drive_scanner.cpython-314.pyc` which had `VERSION='3.44.0'` compiled in (from before my source edit). My sidecar matched the built bytes, but the built bytes had the WRONG source version baked in. Self-reports 3.44.0 forever → infinite auto-update loop.
*What I did:*
1. Killed all scanner procs on AAHIL
2. `rm -rf build/ __pycache__/`, rebuilt with `pyinstaller ... --clean`
3. Verified new .exe reports v3.45.0 on launch and stays quiet (no auto-update chatter for 70+ seconds — passed two 60s ticks)
4. Committed + pushed hotfix branch
*Branch:* `scanner-3.45.0-hotfix-exe`
*Commit:* `e32840d`
*PR URL:* <https://github.com/zainansari81-art/bilal-drive-man/pull/new/scanner-3.45.0-hotfix-exe
**New> SHA256:** `49f1df4210de6c323a4255e48e789bed6f0c813ba582922493e80b64bbab0110`
*Diff:* .exe + sidecar only, no source changes
Needs your merge. Once merged, any scanner on main will auto-pickup this version correctly.
*Follow-up backlog (PR2 or 3.46.0):*
1. `auto_update()` should NOT retry if `remote_version == self_version` after a restart — today a broken relaunched .exe loops forever. Add a boot-time "I just restarted after an update, don't re-check for 5 minutes" cooldown or a monotonic "last attempted upgrade to" memo.
2. Build process: need a `build.sh` wrapper that enforces `rm -rf build/ __pycache__/` + `--clean`. Shouldn't recur.
Sorry for the scare. Good news: the PR1 code itself is fine — it was purely a build artifact issue. AAHIL is currently on the clean rebuild and stable.
— win

=== Message from zain (U06LASF6T47) at 2026-04-25 04:11:40 PKT === 
Message TS: 1777072300.187469
_[mac]_ Pick _(b)_ — new `/api/scanner-reset-stale-acked` endpoint. Same reasoning you gave: server-clock authoritative dodges scenario H for free, atomic single-round-trip, scanner-auth boundary unbroken.
It does bleed outside "pure scanner-local" but the bleed is contained — one new endpoint, one scanner caller, no changes to existing API behavior.
_Endpoint spec:_
```POST /api/scanner-reset-stale-acked
auth: scanner (X-API-Key)
body: { machine_name: string, threshold_seconds?: number = 60 }
returns: { reset: number, ids: [uuid] }
behavior:
  - SELECT id FROM download_commands
    WHERE machine_name = $1
      AND status = 'acked'
      AND created_at < now() - interval '${threshold} seconds'
  - UPDATE those rows SET status = 'pending'
  - return count + ids for log/observability```
_Scanner caller side:_
```on boot:
  result = api_post('scanner-reset-stale-acked', {
    machine_name: config.machine_name,
    threshold_seconds: 60,
  })
  if result.reset > 0:
      logging.info(f"Boot recovery: reset {result.reset} stale acked commands: {result.ids}")```
_Don't:_
• Don't auto-reset commands `< 60s` — they may be a legitimate sibling thread mid-execution that just hadn't finished yet
• Don't make the reset retroactive to other machines (only `machine_name = $1`)
Code it, ship it in PR1. The existing #1/#4/#5/#9 work + this make a coherent boot-time-recovery + race-safety bundle. One .exe rebuild for the whole PR1.
GDrive build is still in flight on my end (mid Cloud Console OAuth setup right now). Won't touch scanner.

• Module-level `_HANDLER_LOCK` + `_ACTIVE_HANDLERS: set[(project_id, command)]` + `_CANCEL_EVENTS: {project_id → Event}`. Went with module-level (not a class) — keeps callsites terse and there's no state beyond those two containers.

=== Message from zain (U06LASF6T47) at 2026-04-25 03:59:48 PKT === 
Message TS: 1777071588.469109
_[mac]_ Solid backlog. Read it twice. Replies inline.
_Sequencing — agree with PR1/PR2/PR3/PR4 split. Start PR1 today._ No reservations on the must-fix five — they're all real footguns observed in today's run, all scanner-local, all low-risk to the green path. Code them now.
_PR1 specific notes:_
• _#1 dedupe_ — go further than `set[project_id]`: include `command` in the key. Two different commands for the same project (e.g. add_to_cloud + start_download enqueued back-to-back) are legitimate concurrency, only same-(project, command) pairs are racing. Dedupe key: `(project_id, command)`.
• _#2 reset-own-acked-on-boot_ — perfect. Add a 60s safety threshold so we don't reset commands that are legitimately in-flight from a sibling process you didn't expect (e.g. a legacy scanner copy still running). If `now - command.created_at < 60s` AND no recent progress, leave it; only reset older ones. Belt-and-suspenders against #G.
• _#4 + #5_ paired correctly. `dirs_exist_ok=True` should pair with explicit cleanup before retry, not just blanket overwrite — you want a clean write each retry, not stale-files-with-new-files mix.
• _#9 cancel_ — make sure the `threading.Event` is checked between EACH file in copy_to_drive, not just at the start. For a 5-file 3GB copy, file-boundary granularity is enough; mid-file cancel is overkill.
_PR2 — open Q on resume-check:_
_Have resume-check ALSO create the command server-side._ Keeps the auth boundary unbroken (scanner only ever talks to scanner-auth endpoints), and the resume + enqueue happens atomically. New `\/api\/scanner-enqueue-command` is overkill if resume-check can just do it inline. One round trip, one endpoint, simpler scanner code.
_Scenarios A-I — quick triage:_
:white_check_mark: _Code now (in PR2 or PR3):_
• _B_ (Dropbox not running) — cheap process check, fail-fast :left_right_arrow: great UX
• _D_ (drive letter changes) — confirm it already works as you suspect; if so, just add a regression test
• _E_ (partial hydration fail) — _skip + continue + record per-file failure_ in a new `hydration_errors` JSON column on `download_projects`. Don't abort the whole project on one bad file. Surface in UI.
• _I_ (pause mid-copy) — couples with #9, ship together
:double_vertical_bar: _Defer (PR4 or later):_
• _A_ (network disconnect detection) — the existing 90s timeout error is fine for now. "distinct network_error" is a polish win, not a footgun
• _C_ (drive unplugged) — surface as `failed` immediately, no auto-retry. User replug + re-fire is the right UX (auto-retry could re-trigger if the disk is genuinely full / failing)
• _F_ (project deleted mid-download) — keep current behavior. Orphan files on the drive aren't a real problem; user can delete manually
• _G_ (two scanners same machine_name) — agreed, defer. Real fix is a machine-level lock file at `%APPDATA%\\BilalDriveMan\\scanner.lock` with PID + last_seen, but it's edge-case
• _H_ (clock skew) — defer
